question:
I. Compliance with duties of information
II. Summary 
Part A – Information about the issuer of the asset-referenced token
Part AA - information on other persons offering to the public or seeking admission to trading of asset-referenced tokens other than the issuer and on other persons drawing up the crypto-asset white paper in accordance with Article 19(1), second subparagraph, of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114
Part B - Information about the asset-referenced token 
Part C - Information about the offer to the public of the asset-referenced token or its admission to trading 
Part D - Information on the rights and obligations attached to the asset-referenced token 
Part E - Information on the underlying technology 
Part F - Information on the risks 
Part G - Information on the reserve of assets 
Part H – Information on the sustainability indicators in relation to adverse impact on the climate and other environment-related adverse impacts
What is the notification date?

The asset-referenced token referred to in this crypto-asset white paper may lose its value in part or in full, may not always be transferable and may not be liquid. The asset-refenced token referred to in this crypto-asset white paper is not covered by the investor compensation schemes under Directive 97/9/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council or the deposit guarantee schemes under Directive 2014/49/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council.’

‘This crypto-asset white paper complies with Title III of Regulation (EU) 2023/1114 of the European Parliament and of the Council and to the best of the knowledge of the management body, the information presented in this crypto-asset white paper is fair, clear and not misleading and the crypto-asset white paper makes no omission likely to affect its import.’

‘Warning This summary should be read as an introduction to the crypto-asset white paper. The prospective holder should base any decision to purchase this asset-referenced token on the content of the crypto-asset white paper as a whole and not on the summary alone.The offer to the public of this crypto-asset does not constitute an offer or solicitation to purchase financial instruments and any such offer or solicitation can be made only by means of a prospectus or other offer documents pursuant to the applicable national law. This crypto-asset white paper does not constitute a prospectus as referred to in Regulation (EU) 2017/1129 of the European Parliament and of the Council or any other offer document pursuant to Union or national law.’

What specific legal rights do token holders have in relation to the asset-referenced token (e.g. redemption, claims on reserves)?
Are there any differences in rights between types or classes of holders? If yes, what are the reasons for such differences, and are they applied in a non-discriminatory way?
What legal or contractual mechanisms ensure that these rights are enforceable under relevant national laws?
What types of assets back the token (e.g. fiat currencies, commodities, crypto-assets, government bonds)? Please list each one.
What proportion or percentage of the reserve is allocated to each referenced asset?
Are there any restrictions or conditions on holding or changing the composition of these referenced assets?
What is the nominal or market value of the reserve assets held to back the token?
What is the amount or value that each token holder can claim against the reserve (e.g. per token)?
How often is the size of the reserve updated or re-evaluated?
How is the amount that a holder can claim linked to the value of the referenced assets?
Is the claim fixed, floating, or subject to market fluctuations?
Who is responsible for valuing the referenced assets and the related claims (internal team or independent third party)?
What methodology is used to determine the fair value of the claim?
Are independent auditors, valuation agents, or other third parties involved in verifying the claim value?
Are there any limitations, conditions, or exceptions to the claim (e.g. thresholds, redemption limits)?
What mechanisms or arrangements are in place to ensure holders can buy/sell tokens without significant delay or price impact?
Are there designated market makers, exchanges, or automated systems that support liquidity?
Is the issuer obligated to provide or support secondary market liquidity directly or through partners?
Who are the named entities responsible for providing liquidity (e.g. market makers, platforms, counterparties)?
What are their roles, and under what agreements or terms do they operate?
What is the designated contact point (email, phone, postal address) for submitting complaints related to the token or issuer?
What are the steps a token holder must follow to submit a complaint?
Is there a formal dispute resolution mechanism in place for token holders? If yes, please describe it.
What specific rights do token holders have if the issuer is unable to fulfil its obligations (e.g. repayment, asset backing)?
How are token holders protected in the event of the issuer’s insolvency or bankruptcy?
Are there any mechanisms in place that allow holders to claim or recover value if the issuer defaults?
What rights do token holders have when the recovery plan is put into effect?
How can holders participate in or influence decisions related to the recovery plan?
What rights do token holders have when the redemption plan is activated?
How are holders informed about the redemption process and their options?
Can holders choose how they want to receive value when redeeming tokens (e.g. cash vs assets)?
If no choice is offered, what is the default redemption form?
In what form can tokens be redeemed (e.g. fiat currency, underlying assets)?
What is the official currency used for redemption (e.g. EUR, USD)?
Can holders choose between different transfer methods when redeeming tokens?
If only one method is offered, what is it and why?
How are redeemed assets or funds transferred to the holder (e.g. SEPA, SWIFT, blockchain transfer)?
Are there any operational steps or requirements holders must follow to receive their redeemed value?
What is the official currency in which redemptions are made (e.g. EUR, USD)?
Is the redemption currency fixed or dependent on holder location or preference?

What are the risks related to the reserve of assets, when the 
issuer is not able to fulfil its obligations?
What are the main financial risks related to the issuer’s treasury or financial reserves, including risks from insufficient revenue or unexpected shortfalls?
How does the issuer manage internal efficiency and resource allocation, and what risks arise from potential mismanagement or inefficient use of resources?
What internal control risks exist, such as those affecting operational integrity, fraud prevention, error management, and operational efficiency?
What governance practices are in place, and what risks are associated with transparency, decision-making processes, and the balance between centralisation and decentralisation?
Which key partnerships does the issuer rely on for operations and strategy, and what risks could arise from disruptions or failures in these partnerships?
What regulatory risks affect the issuer, including compliance challenges, changes in laws, and potential legal issues?

Briefly introduce the context, mentioning if
the oﬀer to the public has already passed
and the relevance of trading risks.]

Irreversibility of Transactions:
○
Describe if payments can be
reversed, deleted, or frozen by the
network or governing body.
○
Describe if payments made under
coercion, deceit, mistakes, or
unlawfully are irreversible, similar to
physical cash.
○
Describe if courts can mandate
compensatory transactions, but lack
the technical means to enforce such
orders without private keys.

Standard Trading Risks:
○
Pricing Volatility: Crypto-asset prices
are susceptible to significant
fluctuations due to market dynamics.

Liquidity Conditions: Insuﬃcient liquidity on
exchanges or trading platforms could lead to
large price swings, impacting the ability to
sell assets without significant price
concessions.

Market Demand: There is a risk that the DLT
may not achieve expected levels of market
adoption, impacting the utility and value of
the asset.

Impact of Large Transactions: Large
transactions by major holders could
significantly influence the price, leading to
potential manipulation or unintended price
destabilisation.

Broader Economic Conditions: Wider
economic downturns or financial market
instability could lead to decreased
investment in crypto-assets, adversely
aﬀecting their price and liquidity

Regulatory Changes: Evolving regulatory
landscapes may impact the oﬀer and trading
of crypto-assets.

Mitigation Strategies: Use of intermediaries,
particularly regulated ones with robust
customer support, can help mitigate some of
these risks, although it reintroduces
conventional risks associated with
dependence on third parties.
What are the key risks associated with holding and trading crypto-assets?
How susceptible is the crypto-asset to significant price fluctuations due to market volatility?
What liquidity risks exist, including challenges in buying or selling without large price concessions?
What risks of market manipulation exist from large holders or coordinated activities?
What broader market risks could affect the value and stability of the crypto-asset?
What risks are associated with the irreversibility of blockchain transactions?
What risks arise from private key management, including loss, theft, or improper handling?
What custodial risks exist when relying on third-party custodians for private key management?
What privacy risks arise from the public recording of transactions on the blockchain and potential exposure of user identities?

Provide a brief overview of the technology-related risks associated with the crypto-asset project.
Describe the risk of network disruption or manipulation if a majority of the networks computational power or stake is compromised.
Highlight vulnerabilities related to network proxies and specific services, such as potential exploits in smart contracts or APIs.
Explain the risk of software bugs and defects in new releases or updates that could compromise system functionality, security, and user trust.
Discuss risks associated with updates to the protocol, including the potential for introducing vulnerabilities or disruptions.
Describe the risks associated with current cryptographic methods, including the potential future threat posed by quantum computing.
Highlight the risks and technical challenges associated with scaling the network, such as implementing sharding or other scaling solutions.
Discuss the importance of interoperability with other blockchain projects and traditional systems, and the risks of delays in achieving it.
Describe other conceivable attacks, such as denial-of-service attacks or network partitioning, which could disrupt network communication.
Provide a brief overview of the mitigation measures in place to address various risks associated with the crypto-asset project.
What mechanisms are used to ensure that the value of the reserve consistently matches or supports the value of the claims attached to the ART (e.g. 1:1 pegging, overcollateralisation)?
Are there automated or algorithmic tools in place to maintain this alignment (e.g. smart contracts, rebalancing protocols)?
What happens if the value of the reserve deviates from the value of the claims (e.g. corrective actions, buffer usage)?
